configuration.name: api_key
configuration.additionalinfo: The API key for authenticating with the KOI API. See the help section for instructions on creating an API key.

configuration.name: event_types_to_fetch
configuration.additionalinfo: Select which event types to fetch from KOI.

configuration.name: audit_types_filter
configuration.additionalinfo: Filter audit logs by type(s). If not specified, all audit log types will be fetched.

configuration.name: max_fetch
configuration.display: Maximum number of events per fetch
configuration.additionalinfo: Maximum number of events to fetch per type per fetch cycle (default: 5000).

commands.name: koi-get-events
commands.description: Gets events from KOI. Use this command for development and debugging only, as it may produce duplicate events, exceed API rate limits, or disrupt the fetch mechanism.

arguments.name: event_type
arguments.description: The type of events to retrieve. If not specified, uses the value configured in the integration parameters.

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: The maximum number of events to return per type.

arguments.name: start_time
arguments.description: Filter events created at or after this time. Supports ISO 8601 format or relative time expressions (e.g., "3 days ago", "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z").

arguments.name: end_time
arguments.description: Filter events created at or before this time. Supports ISO 8601 format or relative time expressions (e.g., "now", "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z").

arguments.name: should_push_events
arguments.description: The flag that indicates whether to push events to Cortex XSIAM. Pushing events is supported on Cortex XSIAM only. When set to false, or on non-XSIAM platforms, events are displayed without being pushed.

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the event.
The source log type of the event (Alerts or Audit).
The timestamp of the event in ISO 8601 format.
The creation time of the event (audit logs).

commands.name: koi-policy-list
commands.description: Retrieves a list of all policies. Use 'page' and 'page_size' to fetch a specific page, or use 'limit' to auto-paginate and collect up to the specified number of policies. If 'page' is provided, 'limit' is ignored.

arguments.name: page
arguments.description: Page number for pagination (1-based). When provided, fetches a single page and ignores the limit argument.

arguments.name: page_size
arguments.description: Number of results per page (default: 50, max: 500). Used only in single-page mode together with the page argument.

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: Maximum total number of policies to return (default: 50, max: 1000). When provided without page, auto-paginates to collect up to this many policies.

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the policy.
The name of the policy.
The description of the policy.
The action taken by the policy (e.g., block).
Whether the policy is enabled.
List of group IDs associated with the policy.
The full name of the policy creator.
The creation time of the policy in ISO 8601 format.
The last update time of the policy in ISO 8601 format.

commands.name: koi-allowlist-get
commands.description: Retrieves all items in the allowlist.

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the allowlist item.
The name of the allowlist item.
The display name of the allowlist item.
The marketplace of the allowlist item (e.g., vscode).
The publisher name of the allowlist item.
The package name of the allowlist item.
Notes associated with the allowlist item.
The user who created the allowlist item.
The creation time of the allowlist item in ISO 8601 format.

commands.name: koi-allowlist-items-remove
commands.description: Removes one or more items from the global allowlist. Provide either 'item_id' and 'marketplace' for a single item, or 'items_list_raw_json_entry_id' for bulk removal from a JSON file.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.description: The ID of the item to remove from the allowlist. Required when not using items_list_raw_json_entry_id.

arguments.name: marketplace
arguments.description: The source marketplace of the item. Required when not using items_list_raw_json_entry_id.

arguments.name: created_by
arguments.description: Email of the user who created this entry.

arguments.name: notes
arguments.description: Additional notes about the removal.

arguments.name: items_list_raw_json_entry_id
arguments.description: War Room entry ID of a JSON file containing a list of items to remove. Each item must have "item_id" and "marketplace" fields. Optional fields: "created_by", "notes". When provided, item_id and marketplace arguments are ignored.

commands.name: koi-allowlist-items-add
commands.description: Adds one or more items to the global allowlist. Provide either 'item_id' and 'marketplace' for a single item, or 'items_list_raw_json_entry_id' for bulk addition from a JSON file.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.description: The ID of the item to add to the allowlist. Required when not using items_list_raw_json_entry_id.

arguments.name: marketplace
arguments.description: The source marketplace of the item. Required when not using items_list_raw_json_entry_id.

arguments.name: created_by
arguments.description: Email of the user who created this entry.

arguments.name: notes
arguments.description: Additional notes about the entry.

arguments.name: items_list_raw_json_entry_id
arguments.description: War Room entry ID of a JSON file containing a list of items to add. Each item must have "item_id" and "marketplace" fields. Optional fields: "created_by", "notes". When provided, item_id and marketplace arguments are ignored.

commands.name: koi-blocklist-get
commands.description: Retrieves all items in the blocklist.

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the blocklist item.
The name of the blocklist item.
The display name of the blocklist item.
The marketplace of the blocklist item (e.g., vscode).
The publisher name of the blocklist item.
The package name of the blocklist item.
Notes associated with the blocklist item.
The user who created the blocklist item.
The creation time of the blocklist item in ISO 8601 format.

commands.name: koi-blocklist-items-remove
commands.description: Removes one or more items from the global blocklist. Provide either 'item_id' and 'marketplace' for a single item, or 'items_list_raw_json_entry_id' for bulk removal from a JSON file.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.description: The ID of the item to remove from the blocklist. Required when not using items_list_raw_json_entry_id.

arguments.name: marketplace
arguments.description: The source marketplace of the item. Required when not using items_list_raw_json_entry_id.

arguments.name: created_by
arguments.description: Email of the user who created this entry.

arguments.name: notes
arguments.description: Additional notes about the removal.

arguments.name: items_list_raw_json_entry_id
arguments.description: War Room entry ID of a JSON file containing a list of items to remove. Each item must have "item_id" and "marketplace" fields. Optional fields: "created_by", "notes". When provided, item_id and marketplace arguments are ignored.

commands.name: koi-blocklist-items-add
commands.description: Adds one or more items to the global blocklist. Provide either 'item_id' and 'marketplace' for a single item, or 'items_list_raw_json_entry_id' for bulk addition from a JSON file.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.description: The ID of the item to add to the blocklist. Required when not using items_list_raw_json_entry_id.

arguments.name: marketplace
arguments.description: The source marketplace of the item. Required when not using items_list_raw_json_entry_id.

arguments.name: created_by
arguments.description: Email of the user who created this entry.

arguments.name: notes
arguments.description: Additional notes or justification for blocking the item.

arguments.name: items_list_raw_json_entry_id
arguments.description: War Room entry ID of a JSON file containing a list of items to add. Each item must have "item_id" and "marketplace" fields. Optional fields: "created_by", "notes". When provided, item_id and marketplace arguments are ignored.

commands.name: koi-policy-status-update
commands.description: Enables or disables a policy by ID.

arguments.name: policy_id
arguments.description: The ID of the policy to update.

arguments.name: enabled
arguments.description: Whether to enable (true) or disable (false) the policy.

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the policy.
The name of the policy.
The description of the policy.
The action taken by the policy (e.g., block).
Whether the policy is enabled.
List of group IDs associated with the policy.
The full name of the policy creator.
The creation time of the policy in ISO 8601 format.
The last update time of the policy in ISO 8601 format.

commands.name: koi-inventory-list
commands.description: Retrieves a paginated list of items installed across your organization's endpoints. Supports extensive filtering by marketplace, platform, risk level, publisher, and specific categories.

arguments.name: page
arguments.description: Page number for pagination (1-based). When provided, fetches a single page and ignores the limit argument.

arguments.name: page_size
arguments.description: Number of results per page (default: 50, max: 500). Used in single-page mode with the page argument.

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: Maximum total number of inventory items to return (default: 50, max: 1000). When provided without page, auto-paginates to collect up to this many items.

arguments.name: brew_category_koi
arguments.description: Filter by Homebrew package category (Koi classification).

arguments.name: browser_category_koi
arguments.description: Filter by browser extension category (Koi classification).

arguments.name: chocolatey_category_koi
arguments.description: Filter by Chocolatey package category (Koi classification).

arguments.name: device_id
arguments.description: Filter devices by device ID.

arguments.name: finding_id
arguments.description: Filter devices by finding ID.

arguments.name: first_seen
arguments.description: Filter by first seen date (items first seen on or after this date). ISO 8601 format (e.g., "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z").

arguments.name: ide_category_koi
arguments.description: Filter by IDE extension category (Koi classification).

arguments.name: installation_method
arguments.description: Filter by installation method.

arguments.name: item_display_name
arguments.description: Filter by item display name. Performs case-insensitive partial match.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.description: Filter by item ID.

arguments.name: marketplace
arguments.description: Filter by marketplace.

arguments.name: platform
arguments.description: Filter by platform.

arguments.name: publisher_name
arguments.description: Filter by publisher name. Performs case-insensitive partial match.

arguments.name: risk_level
arguments.description: Filter by risk level.

arguments.name: software_category_koi
arguments.description: Filter by software category (Koi classification).

arguments.name: sort_by
arguments.description: Column to sort by.

arguments.name: sort_direction
arguments.description: Sort direction.

arguments.name: view
arguments.description: Filter by predefined view (marketplace group).

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the inventory item.
The display name of the inventory item.
The marketplace source of the item.
List of platforms where the item is installed.
The publisher name of the item.
The numeric risk score of the item.
The risk level classification of the item.
The version of the item.
The governance status of the item.
The number of endpoints where the item is installed.
The total number of installs for the item.
The date the item was first seen in ISO 8601 format.
The date the item was last seen in ISO 8601 format.
The date the item was last used in ISO 8601 format.
The method used to install the item.
A short description of the item.
Whether the item is a first-party item.
Whether the item is signed.
List of categories the item belongs to.
List of findings associated with the item.
Governance policy details for the item.
The release date of the item. Format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2023-01-15).
The Homebrew package category (Koi classification).
The browser extension category (Koi classification).
The Chocolatey package category (Koi classification).
The IDE extension category (Koi classification).
The software category (Koi classification).

commands.name: koi-inventory-item-get
commands.description: Retrieves comprehensive details for a specific software item, extension, or package using its unique identifier, marketplace, and version.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.description: Unique identifier for the item.

arguments.name: marketplace
arguments.description: The marketplace where the item is hosted.

arguments.name: version
arguments.description: The specific version of the item to retrieve.

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the inventory item.
The display name of the inventory item.
The marketplace source of the item.
List of platforms where the item is installed.
The publisher name of the item.
The numeric risk score of the item.
The risk level classification of the item.
The version of the item.
The governance status of the item.
The number of endpoints where the item is installed.
The total number of installs for the item.
The method used to install the item.
Whether the item is a first-party item.
Whether the item is signed.
The date the item was first seen in ISO 8601 format.
The date the item was last seen in ISO 8601 format.
The date the item was last used in ISO 8601 format.
The release date of the item. Format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2023-01-15).
A short description of the item.
List of categories the item belongs to.
List of findings associated with the item including severity and evidence.
Governance policy details for the item.
The Homebrew package category (Koi classification).
The browser extension category (Koi classification).
The Chocolatey package category (Koi classification).
The IDE extension category (Koi classification).
The software category (Koi classification).

commands.name: koi-inventory-search
commands.description: Searches inventory items using advanced query builder filters. Provide a filter via 'filter_json' (inline JSON string) or 'filter_raw_json_entry_id' (War Room file entry ID). At least one filter source must be provided.

arguments.name: filter_json
arguments.description: Advanced filter using query builder syntax as a JSON string. Either filter_json or filter_raw_json_entry_id must be provided.

arguments.name: filter_raw_json_entry_id
arguments.description: War Room entry ID of a JSON file containing the filter object. Takes priority over filter_json when both are provided.

arguments.name: page
arguments.description: Page number for pagination (1-based). When provided, fetches a single page and ignores the limit argument.

arguments.name: page_size
arguments.description: Number of results per page (default: 50, max: 500). Used in single-page mode with the page argument.

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: Maximum total number of inventory items to return (default: 50, max: 1000). When provided without page, auto-paginates to collect up to this many items.

arguments.name: sort_by
arguments.description: Column to sort by.

arguments.name: sort_direction
arguments.description: Sort direction.

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the inventory item.
The display name of the inventory item.
The marketplace source of the item.
List of platforms where the item is installed.
The publisher name of the item.
The numeric risk score of the item.
The risk level classification of the item.
The version of the item.
The governance status of the item.
The number of endpoints where the item is installed.
The total number of installs for the item.
The date the item was first seen in ISO 8601 format.
The date the item was last seen in ISO 8601 format.
The date the item was last used in ISO 8601 format.
The method used to install the item.
A short description of the item.
Whether the item is a first-party item.
Whether the item is signed.
List of categories the item belongs to.
List of findings associated with the item.
Governance policy details for the item.
The release date of the item. Format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2023-01-15).
The Homebrew package category (Koi classification).
The browser extension category (Koi classification).
The Chocolatey package category (Koi classification).
The IDE extension category (Koi classification).
The software category (Koi classification).

commands.name: koi-inventory-item-endpoints-list
commands.description: Retrieves a paginated list of endpoints that have a specific item installed.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.description: Unique identifier for the item.

arguments.name: marketplace
arguments.description: The marketplace where the item is hosted.

arguments.name: version
arguments.description: The specific version of the item.

arguments.name: page
arguments.description: Page number for pagination (1-based). When provided, fetches a single page and ignores the limit argument.

arguments.name: page_size
arguments.description: Number of results per page (default: 50, max: 500). Used in single-page mode with the page argument.

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: Maximum total number of endpoints to return (default: 50, max: 1000). When provided without page, auto-paginates to collect up to this many endpoints.

outputs.description: The unique identifier of the endpoint device.
The hostname of the endpoint.
The operating system of the endpoint.
The platform where the item is installed on this endpoint.
The serial number of the endpoint device.
The last logged on user of the endpoint.
The activation status of the endpoint.
The installation path of the item on the endpoint.
The date the item was first seen on this endpoint in ISO 8601 format.
The date the item was last seen on this endpoint in ISO 8601 format.
